test_cases.name: happy_path_postgresql_new_schema
test_cases.scenario: User provides a complete SQL DDL schema for PostgreSQL and requests migration scripts, seed data, and ERD documentation for a new database.

input.schema_definition: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP); CREATE TABLE posts (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id), title VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, content TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP); CREATE TABLE comments (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, post_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES posts(id), user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id), content TEXT NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill generates migration scripts for all three tables in correct dependency order, creates seed data for lookup tables, produces a Mermaid ERD showing user-post-comment relationships, and generates complete markdown documentation with setup instructions for PostgreSQL.

test_cases.name: happy_path_mysql_upgrade_migration
test_cases.scenario: User provides both current and target schemas in SQL format for MySQL and requests upgrade migration scripts with rollback procedures.

input.schema_definition: CREATE TABLE products (id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, price DECIMAL(10,2), category_id INT, FOREIGN KEY (category_id) REFERENCES categories(id)); CREATE TABLE categories (id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL UNIQUE);
input.current_schema: CREATE TABLE products (id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, price DECIMAL(10,2));

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill identifies schema differences (new categories table, new category_id column, new foreign key), generates migration scripts with both forward and rollback SQL, creates seed data for categories, produces PlantUML ERD, and generates HTML documentation with rollback procedures.

test_cases.name: edge_case_json_schema_format
test_cases.scenario: User provides schema in JSON format instead of SQL DDL, testing parser flexibility and format conversion.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill successfully parses JSON schema format, generates SQLite-compatible migration scripts without seed data, produces ASCII-art ERD, and validates all outputs for consistency.

test_cases.name: edge_case_complex_schema_split_erd
test_cases.scenario: User provides a schema with 25 tables and complex relationships, triggering ERD splitting into multiple diagrams.

input.schema_definition: CREATE TABLE organizations (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255)); CREATE TABLE departments (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, org_id INTEGER REFERENCES organizations(id), name VARCHAR(255)); CREATE TABLE employees (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, dept_id INTEGER REFERENCES departments(id), name VARCHAR(255)); CREATE TABLE projects (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, org_id INTEGER REFERENCES organizations(id), name VARCHAR(255)); CREATE TABLE assignments (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, employee_id INTEGER REFERENCES employees(id), project_id INTEGER REFERENCES projects(id)); CREATE TABLE tasks (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, assignment_id INTEGER REFERENCES assignments(id), project_id INTEGER REFERENCES projects(id), title VARCHAR(255)); CREATE TABLE subtasks (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, task_id INTEGER REFERENCES tasks(id), title VARCHAR(255)); CREATE TABLE comments (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, task_id INTEGER REFERENCES tasks(id), employee_id INTEGER REFERENCES employees(id), content TEXT); CREATE TABLE attachments (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, task_id INTEGER REFERENCES tasks(id), file_path VARCHAR(500)); CREATE TABLE time_logs (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, assignment_id INTEGER REFERENCES assignments(id), hours DECIMAL(5,2)); CREATE TABLE status_codes (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, code VARCHAR(50), description VARCHAR(255)); CREATE TABLE priorities (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, level VARCHAR(50)); CREATE TABLE task_status (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, task_id INTEGER REFERENCES tasks(id), status_id INTEGER REFERENCES status_codes(id)); CREATE TABLE task_priority (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, task_id INTEGER REFERENCES tasks(id), priority_id INTEGER REFERENCES priorities(id)); CREATE TABLE notifications (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, employee_id INTEGER REFERENCES employees(id), message TEXT); CREATE TABLE audit_logs (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, entity_type VARCHAR(100), entity_id INTEGER, action VARCHAR(50), timestamp TIMESTAMP); CREATE TABLE permissions (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, role_name VARCHAR(100), resource VARCHAR(255)); CREATE TABLE role_assignments (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, employee_id INTEGER REFERENCES employees(id), role_name VARCHAR(100)); CREATE TABLE budgets (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, project_id INTEGER REFERENCES projects(id), amount DECIMAL(12,2)); CREATE TABLE expenses (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, project_id INTEGER REFERENCES projects(id), amount DECIMAL(12,2)); CREATE TABLE vendors (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255)); CREATE TABLE vendor_contracts (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, vendor_id INTEGER REFERENCES vendors(id), project_id INTEGER REFERENCES projects(id)); CREATE TABLE milestones (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, project_id INTEGER REFERENCES projects(id), name VARCHAR(255), due_date DATE); CREATE TABLE deliverables (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, milestone_id INTEGER REFERENCES milestones(id), description TEXT);

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill detects schema complexity exceeding 20 tables, generates multiple ERD sections (e.g., organizational structure, project management, financial tracking), creates a master ERD showing section relationships, generates all migration scripts in correct dependency order, and produces comprehensive documentation.

test_cases.name: error_handling_circular_foreign_key
test_cases.scenario: User provides a schema with circular foreign key dependencies (A->B->C->A), which should be detected and reported with remediation guidance.

input.schema_definition: CREATE TABLE table_a (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, b_id INTEGER REFERENCES table_b(id)); CREATE TABLE table_b (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, c_id INTEGER REFERENCES table_c(id)); CREATE TABLE table_c (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, a_id INTEGER REFERENCES table_a(id));

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill detects circular dependency using depth-first search, reports the cycle (table_a->table_b->table_c->table_a), flags as HIGH severity, suggests remediation options (remove one FK or create junction table), and returns validation report with FAIL status until user confirms resolution.

test_cases.name: error_handling_missing_primary_key
test_cases.scenario: User provides a schema where one or more tables lack a primary key, which should be detected and flagged as a critical issue.

input.schema_definition: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255)); CREATE TABLE audit_log (user_id INTEGER, action VARCHAR(100), timestamp TIMESTAMP);

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill detects that audit_log table lacks a primary key during Phase 0 pre-flight check, flags as HIGH severity issue, suggests adding a surrogate primary key (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY), and returns FAIL status in preflight_status and validation_report.

test_cases.name: error_handling_unrecognized_schema_format
test_cases.scenario: User provides an unsupported schema format (e.g., 'xml') that is not in the supported list, triggering an abort response.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill validates schema_format in Phase 0 pre-flight check, detects that 'xml' is not in the supported list (sql, json, yaml, markdown), aborts execution, and returns error message listing all supported formats.

test_cases.name: quality_gate_seed_data_fk_constraint_violation
test_cases.scenario: User provides seed data that violates foreign key constraints (orphaned records), which should be detected during validation and cause abort.

input.schema_definition: CREATE TABLE categories (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100)); CREATE TABLE products (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, category_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES categories(id), name VARCHAR(255));

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill generates seed data scripts but during Phase 7 validation detects that product with category_id=999 references non-existent category, reports FK constraint violation, aborts with FAIL status, and requests user to fix seed_data_spec.

test_cases.name: quality_gate_migration_down_script_reversibility
test_cases.scenario: Skill generates migration scripts and validates that down scripts are true inverses of up scripts, ensuring rollback capability.

input.schema_definition: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, age INTEGER);
input.current_schema: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL);

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill generates migration to add age column with up script (ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN age INTEGER) and down script (ALTER TABLE users DROP COLUMN age), validates during Phase 7 that down script is true inverse of up script, and confirms reversibility in validation_report.

test_cases.name: quality_gate_documentation_completeness
test_cases.scenario: Skill generates documentation and validates that all required sections are present and non-empty, including troubleshooting with minimum 3 issues.

input.schema_definition: CREATE TABLE orders (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, customer_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES customers(id), total DECIMAL(10,2)); CREATE TABLE customers (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, email VARCHAR(255));

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill generates complete documentation package with all sections (readme, schema_overview, migration_guide, erd_visual, metadata), includes troubleshooting section with at least 3 common issues and solutions, provides example commands for PostgreSQL, and validation confirms all sections are present and non-empty.

quality_gates.gate: Phase 0: Schema format recognized and supported
Phase 1: All foreign key dependencies form a DAG (no circular dependencies)
Phase 2: No circular dependencies in migration order
Phase 3: Down scripts are true inverses of up scripts (reversible)
Phase 4: All seed data respects foreign key constraints
Phase 5: All tables appear in ERD (or in ERD sections if split)
Phase 6: All documentation sections present and non-empty
Phase 7: No critical errors; validation report is PASS or PASS WITH WARNINGS

error_handlers.error: Phase 0: All tables lack primary keys
Phase 1: Schema has circular foreign key dependencies
Phase 1: Schema has tables without primary keys
Phase 4: Seed data violates foreign key constraints
Phase 5: ERD is too complex (more than 20 tables)
